suggestion: This proposal has fundamental issues for a top ML conference: (1) it is not a machine learning paper—no ML methodology is proposed; (2) it instructs rejection of established physics (SR, Michelson-Morley) which is scientifically untenable; (3) it relies on Hermetic mystical principles rather than testable hypotheses. For a viable ML paper, consider: 'Geometric Deep Learning for Crystal Structure-Property Prediction: Exploiting Point Group Symmetries and Topological Descriptors.' This would use graph neural networks or equivariant architectures to learn structure-property relationships in materials, incorporating symmetry-aware representations (which could include icosahedral symmetries relevant to quasicrystals) and topological data analysis features. This is publishable, testable, and genuinely novel if the architecture design is thoughtful.
